questionText: A particle traces the path $x = 5\cos t$, $y = 2\sin t$ for $0 \le t < 2\pi$. Find the values of $t$ at which the particle is moving momentarily horizontally, and the values of $t$ at which it is moving momentarily vertically, giving the coordinates of the point in each case.

steps.explanation: Moving horizontally means there is no vertical velocity, so $\dot{y}=0$. Moving vertically means there is no horizontal velocity, so $\dot{x}=0$. Finding these tells us where the tangent to the path is flat or upright.

steps.description: Differentiate x with respect to t
steps.explanation: Differentiating $5\cos t$ gives the horizontal velocity component of the particle.

steps.description: Differentiate y with respect to t
steps.explanation: Differentiating $2\sin t$ gives the vertical velocity component.

steps.description: Set the vertical velocity to zero
steps.explanation: For horizontal motion the vertical velocity must vanish, so we solve $\cos t = 0$.
These are the solutions of $\cos t = 0$ inside the interval $0 \le t < 2\pi$.

steps.description: Find the points of horizontal motion
steps.explanation: Substituting each time back into the position equations gives the top and bottom of the ellipse, where the path is momentarily flat.

steps.description: Set the horizontal velocity to zero
steps.explanation: For vertical motion the horizontal velocity must vanish, so we solve $\sin t = 0$.
These are the solutions of $\sin t = 0$ inside $0 \le t < 2\pi$.

steps.description: Find the points of vertical motion
steps.explanation: These are the far right and far left of the ellipse, where the path is momentarily upright.
The two flat points sit at the top and bottom of the ellipse and the two upright points at its sides, which matches the shape of the path.

workedSolution.finalAnswer: The particle moves momentarily horizontally at $t=\dfrac{\pi}{2}$ (point $(0,2)$) and $t=\dfrac{3\pi}{2}$ (point $(0,-2)$); it moves momentarily vertically at $t=0$ (point $(5,0)$) and $t=\pi$ (point $(-5,0)$).
